facts.context: Which of the following illustrates the demand curve facing an oligopolist when rival firms follow a price decrease but not a price increase?
facts.truth: A demand curve consisting of two discontinuous segments.
distractors: Perfectly horizontal demand curve.
Highly inelastic demand curve.
Highly elastic demand curve.

facts.context: The industry that makes plastic army figures uses a small fraction of the plastic demanded for all purposes. On this basis, we can conclude that the army-figures industry is most likely a(n)
facts.truth: constant-cost industry
distractors: increasing-cost industry
decreasing-cost industry
profit-making industry

facts.context: Which of the following is most likely an example of production inputs that can be adjusted in the long run, but not in the short run?
facts.truth: The size of a McDonald’s kitchen.
distractors: Amount of wood used to make a desk.
Number of pickles put on a sandwich.
Number of teacher’s assistants in local high schools.

facts.context: Ray and Dorothy can both cook and can both pull weeds in the garden on a Saturday afternoon. For every hour of cooking, Ray can pull 50 weeds and Dorothy can pull 100 weeds. Based on this information,
facts.truth: Ray cooks, since he has comparative advantage in cooking.
distractors: Ray pulls weeds, since he has absolute advantage in cooking.
Dorothy pulls weeds, since she has absolute advantage in cooking.
Dorothy cooks, since she has comparative advantage in cooking.

facts.context: Marginal cost (MC) is equal to average variable cost (AVC) and average total cost (ATC) when:
facts.truth: MC intersects AVC and ATC at their minimum points.
distractors: marginal cost (MC) intersects AVC and ATC at their maximum points.
AVC and ATC intersect MC at its maximum point.
AVC and ATC intersect MC at its minimum point.

facts.context: In the design of a competitive market system, which of the following does NOT describe how resources are allocated?
facts.truth: Active government ownership and direction of production.
distractors: Price signals that guide producers on what, when, how, and for whom to produce goods and services.
Voluntary market exchange between buyers and sellers.
Distribution of income and goods on the basis of contribution or productivity.

facts.context: A production possibility frontier will be a straight line when
facts.truth: resources are not specialized
distractors: efficiency is achieved
the goods on the axes are perfect substitutes in consumption
utility is maximized

facts.context: The market structures of perfect competition and monopolistic competition share which of the following characteristics?
facts.truth: Ease of entry and exit in the long run
distractors: Homogenous products
Perfectly elastic demand for the firm's product
Long-run positive profits

facts.context: Which of these situations is not an example of price discrimination?
facts.truth: Brent works nights, so he chooses to buy bread at 7 a.m. rather than at 7 p.m.
distractors: Bob and Nancy each receive a "$1 off" coupon in the mail, but Bob redeems it while Nancy does not.
Katie buys 12 Cokes for $3, and Josh buys one Coke at a time for $1.
Velma likes to go to the movies at the lower afternoon matinee price, and Rosemary would rather pay more for the evening show.

facts.context: If a business wants to increase its revenue and it knows that the demand price elasticity of its product is equal to 0.78, it should
facts.truth: increase price because demand is inelastic
distractors: decrease price because demand is elastic
decrease price because demand is unit elastic
decrease price because demand is inelastic

facts.context: Which of the following is a characteristic of monopoly?
facts.truth: A single firm in the industry.
distractors: Price equal to marginal revenue.
Perfectly elastic demand curve.
Weak barriers to entry.

facts.context: Firms with the following market structure(s) maximize profits by producing where marginal cost equals marginal revenue, if at all. I. Perfect competition II. Oligopoly III. Monopoly IV. Monopolistic competition
facts.truth: I, II, III, and IV
distractors: I only
I and II only
I and III only

facts.context: Relatively free or easy entry (low or nonexistent barriers to entry) is best matched by which of the following?
facts.truth: More consumer choices, greater price elasticity of demand, more competitors.
distractors: More consumer choices, lower price elasticity of demand, more competitors.
More consumer choices, greater price elasticity of demand, fewer competitors.
Fewer consumer choices, lower price elasticity of demand, fewer competitors.

facts.context: Firms maximize their profits by producing a level of output at which
facts.truth: MC = MR.
distractors: MC = AFC.
P = ATC.
MR= AVC.

facts.context: When a monopolist has maximized profit,
facts.truth: price is set above marginal cost, creating allocative inefficiency.
distractors: price is set equal to marginal cost, creating zero economic profit.
output is set where price is equal to average total cost.
any short-run profit will be eliminated through the long-run entry of new firms.

facts.context: Price times quantity measures
facts.truth: total revenue.
distractors: the international trade gap.
the budget deficit.
price elasticity of demand.

facts.context: The elasticity of supply is typically greater when
facts.truth: producers have more time to respond to price changes.
distractors: producers have fewer alternative goods to produce.
producers have less time to respond to price changes.
producers are operating near the limits of their production.

facts.context: The law of increasing costs is useful in describing
facts.truth: a concave production possibility frontier.
distractors: a demand curve.
a marginal benefit curve.
a linear production possibility frontier.

facts.context: Which of the following is the best example of a negative externality and the appropriate plan for eliminating it?
facts.truth: Air pollution from a power plant is blowing downwind and harming the trees in your community. Tax the production of electricity.
distractors: Air pollution from a factory blows downwind and harms children in a small community. Tax the citizens of the community.
Your neighbor plants a fragrant blooming cherry tree in her front yard. Give a tree subsidy to your neighbor.
The waste from a hog farm pollutes a neighbor's drinking water. Give a subsidy to the hog farmer.

facts.context: The owner of a competitive firm making zero economic profit
facts.truth: is making exactly what she would make in her next best alternative job
distractors: should consider shutting down because she could make more elsewhere
is making less than normal profits
will most likely make more profits in the long run

facts.context: Which of the following goods is likely to have the most elastic demand curve?
facts.truth: Demand for white Ford minivans
distractors: Demand for automobiles
Demand for Ford automobiles
Demand for American-made automobiles

facts.context: Which of the following is true for a firm operating under perfect competition in the long run?
facts.truth: The firm will realize zero economic profits.
distractors: The firm will engage in non-price competition.
The firm will realize economic profits.
The firm will incur economic losses.

facts.context: When the production or consumption of a good creates a positive externality, it is deemed a market failure because at the market quantity
facts.truth: the marginal social benefit exceeds the marginal social cost.
distractors: the marginal social cost exceeds the marginal social benefit.
society produces too much of the good.
the private benefits from consuming the good exceed the social benefits.

facts.context: Which of the following is not correct about economies of scale?
facts.truth: Economies of scale are associated with the rising or increasing portion of an average total cost (ATC) curve.
distractors: Economies of scale are associated with increases in production of output.
Economies of scale are associated with the declining or decreasing portions of the ATC curve.
Economies of scale result in decreases in per unit average cost.

facts.context: If the perfectly competitive price is currently above minimum ATC, we can expect which of the following events in the long run?
facts.truth: Price falls as firms enter the industry.
distractors: Price rises as firms enter the industry.
Market equilibrium quantity rises as firms exit the industry.
Nothing. The industry is currently in long-run equilibrium.

facts.context: Which of the following is likely to have a demand curve that is the least elastic?
facts.truth: Demand for the monopoly firm's output
distractors: Demand for the perfectly competitive firm's output
Demand for the oligopoly firm's output with a homogenous product
Demand for the oligopoly firm's output with a differentiated product

facts.context: In the same period there is a drought affecting the supply of pineapples and a discovery that may assist in the avoidance of cancer. How will this combination of events affect the equilibrium price and quantity of pineapples?Equilibrium Quantity
facts.truth: Increases         Indeterminate
distractors: Increases         Decreases
Indeterminate         Increases
Decreases         Indeterminate

facts.context: Suppose that there are only two goods: x and y. Which of the following is NOT correct?
facts.truth: One can have comparative advantage in producing both goods.
distractors: One can have both an absolute advantage and a comparative advantage in producing x.
One can have absolute advantage and no comparative advantage in producing x.
One can have comparative advantage and no absolute advantage in producing x.

facts.context: The total utility from sardines is maximized when they are purchased until
facts.truth: marginal utility is zero
distractors: marginal benefit equals marginal cost
consumer surplus is zero
distributive efficiency is achieved

facts.context: Which of the following is true about the relationship of the average total cost (ATC) curve and the marginal cost (MC) curve?
facts.truth: The MC curve intersects the ATC curve at the minimum point of the ATC curve.
distractors: ATC and MC are always equal.
ATC and MC are never equal.
The ATC curve intersects the MC curve at the minimum point of the MC curve.
